analysis.mistake: Two layout/spacing issues passed QA: (1) Images were thin vertical strips on desktop because aspect-auto and h-full utilities had no effect on a constrained container. (2) Excerpt text had no spacing before the CTA button because justify-between was applied to a flex-col column that couldn't justify in the vertical direction.
analysis.expected: User expected images to maintain proper 4:3 proportions at all breakpoints and sufficient spacing (24px) between text group and CTA button, matching Figma design.
analysis.rootCause: QA agent failed to visually compare rendered proportions against Figma design. Both issues are observable by taking a screenshot and comparing side-by-side: (1) image proportions visually distorted vs design, (2) text bunched against button vs design spacing. The issues passed QA because the agent did not perform pixel-level visual comparison of rendered output to Figma. Developer's fix attempts (md:aspect-auto, justify-between) reveal incomplete CSS understanding, but the real systemic failure was QA not catching visual mismatches through screenshot comparison.

suggestions.section: Mode 3: Quality Assurance — Step 3.5: Explicit Pixel Comparison (MANDATORY)
suggestions.content: When verifying image aspect ratios in multi-column layouts (e.g., image panels, card galleries), take screenshots at every breakpoint AND manually measure rendered aspect ratios (width:height visual proportions). Compare to Figma measurements. This catches constraint failures where the image container has conflicting width/height/aspect-ratio rules that distort proportions visually despite correct CSS utilities appearing in code. Use browser DevTools to measure bounding box dimensions if unsure.
suggestions.rationale: Image aspect ratio issues are frequently caused by conflicting parent/child constraints that look correct in code but visually distort the element. Code review alone misses these failures.

suggestions.section: Mode 3: Quality Assurance — Step 3: Detailed Visual Comparison
suggestions.content: For card/panel layouts with multiple text elements (heading, subtext, excerpt, CTA), verify spacing between each element by visual measurement, not by checking for flex/justify utility classes. Spacing can fail due to applied utilities being incompatible with the container layout direction (e.g., justify-between on flex-col). Measure vertical gaps from screenshot and compare to Figma spec for each element pair.
suggestions.rationale: Flex utilities can appear correct in code but fail to apply if the layout direction doesn't match their axis. Visual measurement catches these failures immediately.
